summary: Switched the build to use the Metrowerks 1.3.2 linker while keeping the compiler at MWCC 1.0. The stated motivation was link-time speed: 1.3.2 was faster than the previous linker and less difficult to adopt than 2.7. The change required a small Makefile split between compiler and linker versions plus minor symbol/layout adjustments around C++ exception initialization data, including naming lbl_804D5B40 as fragmentID.
what_changed: Added MWCC_LD_VERSION := 1.3.2 to the Makefile while leaving MWCC_VERSION := 1.0 for compilation.
Renamed the small-data symbol lbl_804D5B40 to fragmentID in asm/sdata.s.
Updated src/runtime/__init_cpp_exceptions.c to reference fragmentID instead of lbl_804D5B40 in declarations, C code, and inline assembly comments/instructions.
Inserted a blank line in asm/extabindex.s before lbl_80005918, likely to preserve or clarify layout under the new linker behavior; the diff does not explain a semantic reason.
smart_moves: Separated compiler and linker version variables instead of changing MWCC_VERSION globally, minimizing risk to compiler codegen while still gaining linker speed.
Kept the linker executable path versioned through Makefile variables, making future linker swaps explicit and easy to audit.
Paired the linker switch with exact matching cleanup in runtime exception data rather than treating build-tool changes as isolated from layout-sensitive symbols.
Promoted lbl_804D5B40 to the meaningful name fragmentID once its role in __register_fragment and __unregister_fragment was clear.
decomp_lessons: Toolchain changes can affect matching and section layout even when no decompiled game logic changes; verify extab/extabindex/sdata-sensitive areas after linker swaps.
It can be useful to decouple MWCC compiler and MWLD linker versions because code generation and linking performance/behavior are separate concerns.
When a linker upgrade is needed for workflow speed, constrain the blast radius by changing only LD first and leaving CC untouched.

Runtime support files such as __init_cpp_exceptions.c may expose useful symbol semantics: a small-data integer initialized/used through __register_fragment and __unregister_fragment is better named fragmentID than left as lbl_804D5B40.
Inline assembly references and extern declarations must be kept in sync with renamed assembly labels.

naming_conventions.reason: The symbol stores the fragment identifier returned by __register_fragment and later passed to __unregister_fragment in the C++ exception runtime.

naming_conventions.name: MWCC_LD_VERSION
naming_conventions.lesson: Use a distinct variable for the linker version when the compiler version remains different.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Kept compiler version at 1.0 to avoid broad codegen changes while using linker 1.3.2 for faster link time.
Updated r13 small-data label references in inline assembly from lbl_804D5B40(r13) to fragmentID(r13) without changing the generated instructions or offsets.
Maintained .incbin-backed assembly sections in extabindex.s and sdata.s while making minimal label/formatting adjustments around affected symbols.
The fragmentID label remains located at the same sdata offset represented by the prior lbl_804D5B40 symbol, preserving references such as lwz/stw via r13.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that changed MWCC_LD_VERSION or linker versions after 1.3.2.
Search for fragmentID to see whether it was later typed, moved to a header, or matched in a fuller runtime decompilation.
Look for PRs discussing linker 2.7 adoption or why it was considered finnicky.
Audit other runtime exception symbols near lbl_80005918 and extabindex_ for naming opportunities.
